item.title: The continued Grift of the United States
item.description: FP Edit: According to some idiot in the comments, it is my fault that I went homeless. It's my fault that government program said that they would help me, when they didn't have the ability to. It's my fault that I am a sucker and a loser. And it's my fault that I made it through my military service and came home from the deployments overseas. My bad, did not know I eas supposed to care. ------- United States has yet to stand up to the tyrannical leader that has trapped them. We do not have hope here, they flaunt their pedophilia in our faces. And laugh at us while many of us go hungry and unhoused.
item.dc:creator: JustGridSquares

item.title: Ok... ok. I'll buy a flashlight.
item.dc:creator: astronomypictures

item.title: A whole decade ago
item.description:
Once upon a time when Imgur hosted summer camp and it was one of the best three day weekends ive ever had
This was back when Imgur was run by Sara and Alan
Upvotes were currency
And dickbutt was king
We met together in the redwoods of California
The place was beautiful
And you had the option of staying in a communal tent with others or bringing and pitching your own tent!
The place was HUGE camp Navarro I believe is the name of the resort
They provided breakfast lunch and dinner for the three days we all were there, from what I remember the food was delicious
The area is designed to bring everyone together with a huge firepit
Plenty of space and seating
And the ingredients for s’mores making provided as well, meant even in the beginning few hours we were already bonding an getting to know one another
Not to mention alcohol was also provided and greatly helped us all become easier acquainted
What a night that first night was and it only got better from there on out!
Bonding!
The mess hall was beautiful all lit up at night and had a huge dance floor and music playing for a 90s dance night
Giant jenga was such a hit and SO FUN! And you knew someone lost even a few yards away with how loud the tower was when it fell!
Made for some fun moments and lots of laughs!
We partied HARD! But it was all in good fun! I believe this man’s name was Andrew and I think he made it a point to remember everyone’s name and I think he succeeded!
It’s wasn’t just drinking tho! The resort was set up with so many places for groups of people to gather and spend time together
Games of all sorts were encouraged to be brought and may people did and had a blast!!
More bonding!
There were also activities planed every day just like summer camp! Sack racing
A rock climbing tower that was a highlight and regularly used for the three days
I mean look how much fun they’re having!!
One of the attendees(not this gentleman) worked for a toy company and he brought three boxes of these cat paws and handed them out to others on the first night!
There was a scavenger hunt that you got awarded a badge for!
These badges!! Lurker badge was the hardest one to get!
They also had shirts and sticker for everyone who went!
One of the things was a pie eating contest!
So much fun and competition!
Going in!!
Looks so yummy!
Some attendees could offer classes on a huge bulletin board, the gentleman in the center of this photo was teaching this group how to dance
There was also archery not pictured and tug of war!!
They had a pajama day!
And different named areas for campers to become even more connected and feel like groups
I think the biggest thing that everyone felt was the connections to each other
Limited internet, games, activities, alcohol ,and food helped us all link up on a different level, it really was like being at summer camp
Look at those smiles!
There were people literally from all over the world. I believe some of the locations included Sweden, Japan, Canada and of course all over the USA! My family and I drove from NY to CA and the road trip there and back plus this three day weekend are memories I’ll treasure forever
If you weren’t there I haven’t even begun to mention all of the things that happened that weekend, from the guided nighttime stargazing trips where we were able to see the Milky Way so clearly it was like you could reach out and touch it, to the art area where you could be creative to your hearts content, to all the memories and discussions that were had. I think the thing that made the biggest impact was each individual that attended. Everyone was their authentic selves, and we left feeling even more like a community but we weren’t just online anymore, we were real, in person. I’ll never forget @CampImgur, I’ll never forget the laughs the connections the community we made. So much has changed in the real world and here on Imgur, but for a few days in time, we were together for real, and I’m forever changed because of it. I also take no credit for any of the pictures and wish I could credit everyone I took them from but I’ve collected them over the past few weeks to create this post. If anything I’ve used is yours please let me know! And please if there is ever a chance of making camp Imgur happen again, let me know!!
item.dc:creator: NIMHspal

item.title: Those were the Days my Friend, it was 1983
item.description:
Billy Joel 1983 Uptown Girl
Cilla 1983 Only a Winter's Tale
David Bowie 1983 China Girl
Donna Summer 1983 She Works Hard For The Money
Frankie Goes to Hollywood 1983 Relax
Nena 1983 99 Luftballons
Quiet Riot 1983 Cum On Feel The Noize
Spandau Ballet 1983 Gold
Stranglers 1983 European Female
Thompson Twins 1983 Hold Me Now
Wham 1983 Club Tropicana
item.dc:creator: SwissScars

item.title: Meet An Engineering Student Who Built His Own Electric Bike Out Of Wood To Commute To Class - Reed Curry And His "Wooden Horse"
item.description: This is the kind of creativity we love to see! When engineering student Reed Curry needed a way to get to and from school in Florida but didn't have a car, he built his own solution. Using materials from his local Home Depot, Curry handcrafted an all-electric wooden motorcycle, which he proudly calls the "Wooden Horse." Made from cedar 2x4s, the bike has been his daily ride for over a year. "This bike isn't a masterpiece, it's not even my main project – it's just my daily commute. I've been riding on this rig for over a year, it's still a work in progress, and I didn't make this with the intention of blowing up," he shared on Instagram. #woodworking #awesome #creativity #wholesome #mildly_interesting
item.dc:creator: OceansRust

item.title: I'm not a punk music fan and even I know this is what they're about!! And the fact that being kind is counter culture now is ridiculous!
item.dc:creator: DumpingSh1tandBeingKind2

item.title: I don't think I'd be leaving.
item.description: I'm a father of 3. I'm from the UK, England, west midlands. We are a small family currently having a rough year with the last couple of months being the roughest. We were suppose to receive not only our payment but our back payment from the government on the 28th of this month and has put us in a very bad situation. It's the summer holidays and I've got next to no time that isn't devoted towards the kids. Update on everything going on. The payment we were meant to receive on the 28th has now been delayed by DWP until the 17th of next month. which has put massive stress on the family with upcoming bills from August such as bills, rent, food and essentials to take care of the kids. Thank you to the people who have helped me get through this either it be with my family or just advice or just positive things. Thank you to the Pizza angels for helping me with grocery vouchers this month. I don't have enough word's to thank the people who have helped and carried my family through this apart from it means the absolute world to me. I've put a goal up to help with the thing's we need for the current month coming up. Thank you from the bottom of my heart if you take 5 minutes to read this. Getting to the finish line means everything to me and the kids. https://ko-fi.com/fatherofthreelions Update - We are currently looking to cover the food this month. Thank you to everyone who has supported me and my family. I cannot thank you enough.
item.dc:creator: thefatherslasthope

item.description: I'm a father of 3. I'm from the UK, England, west midlands. We are a small family currently having a rough year with the last couple of months being the roughest. We were suppose to receive not only our payment but our back payment from the government on the 28th of this month and has put us in a very bad situation. It's the summer holidays and I've got next to no time that isn't devoted towards the kids. Update on everything going on. The payment we were meant to receive on the 28th has now been delayed by DWP until the 17th of next month. which has put massive stress on the family with upcoming bills from August such as bills, rent, food and essentials to take care of the kids. Thank you to the people who have helped me get through this either it be with my family or just advice or just positive things. Thank you to the Pizza angels for helping me with grocery vouchers this month. I don't have enough word's to thank the people who have helped and carried my family through this apart from it means the absolute world to me. I've put a goal up to help with the thing's we need for the current month coming up. Thank you from the bottom of my heart if you take 5 minutes to read this. Getting to the finish line means everything to me and the kids. https://ko-fi.com/fatherofthreelions Update - We are currently looking to cover the food this month. Thank you to everyone who has supported me and my family. I cannot thank you enough.

item.title: Comic Memes Vol 1
item.description: I'm picking back up where I left off clearing out some of the memes I spent a frankly absurd amount of time organizing into folders on my phone. Hope you all enjoyed, as always I'll post the links to my previous posts by category below for anyone who's interested. Star Wars: Part 1: https://imgur.com/gallery/SVzbsKr Part 2: https://imgur.com/gallery/agPBjZY Part 3: https://imgur.com/gallery/3WbYPiO Part 4: https://imgur.com/gallery/D3xLtoh Part 5: https://imgur.com/gallery/WjKSvnE
item.dc:creator: GeeeButtersnaps

item.title: Five Year Plan
item.description:
This comic has been made possible by our supporters on Patreon. While millions enjoy our work for free, it’s your generosity that keeps the gears turning. Thanks to you, we can focus on creating without hustling on the side. We’re endlessly grateful. PS: https://www.patreon.com/join/warandpeas
Meeting you, our dear readers (aka followers aka fans?), at festivals is always a heartwarming experience. There’s something truly special about connecting with people from all corners of the world who find a piece of themselves in our stories. Traveling to different conventions and countries, we’re constantly reminded of the vast, diverse community that stares into our little comic universe. It can be overwhelming at times, the sheer scale of faces and stories, but it’s also incredibly rewarding. Each smile, each shared laugh, and every story you tell us about how our comics touched your life keeps us going. Thank you so much for that. And if we ever appear weird or exhausted, please don’t take it personally, it’s just what we are at times. -------> Where would you like us to go next? Which festival should be at the top of our bucket list? xx Elizabeth and Jonathan
item.dc:creator: Warandpeasofficial

item.title: Yes
item.description: Everyone is equal.
item.dc:creator: thornedrose27

item.title: My Catwoman cosplay transformation - YuzuPyon
item.description:
Source : https://youtube.com/shorts/JmfPDyAjl1U PART 2 BELOW Hello everyone! While I'm busy crafting my big next project, I did this quick Catwoman cosplay project to get something new out in the meantime :) I wanted to showcase that even for a quick easy cosplay project like this one, multiple different skillsets are necessary : patterning, sewing, make-up art, photo lighting set up, modelling, acting and posing! Cosplay always requires many seemingly unrelated skills all at the same time, and that is the beauty of this hobby that makes it so interesting and stimulating in my opinion! :D Feel free to follow my Youtube Channel to see more content like this and catch my upcoming livestream and pattern updates as well :) Wishing you all a great day, Yuzu
Source : https://youtube.com/shorts/JmfPDyAjl1U Hello everyone! While I'm busy crafting my big next project, I did this quick Catwoman cosplay project to get something new out in the meantime :) I wanted to showcase that even for a quick easy cosplay project like this one, multiple different skillsets are necessary : patterning, sewing, make-up art, photo lighting set up, modelling, acting and posing! Cosplay always requires many seemingly unrelated skills all at the same time, and that is the beauty of this hobby that makes it so interesting and stimulating in my opinion! :D Feel free to follow my Youtube Channel to see more content like this and catch my upcoming livestream and pattern updates as well :) Wishing you all a great day, Yuzu
item.dc:creator: yuzupyon

item.title: Heros to Villains
item.description: Fp Edit: it's about 2:00 a.m. where I'm at, and as always I'm sitting here exhausted consumed buying anxiety. Something that is on my mind that has alarm bells ringing for many as well. Is the future requirements of identification for everything from youtube, spotify, to even video games. This is the thing you need to protest. I used to collect intelligence for the United States government. I'm telling you this now this is a huge red flag. Im worried. ------ Some of these were called Heroes. I'm failing to see how any of them live up to the title Two doubled down their support for Trump that you were surprised about? Edit: Shaquille o'neal, has been a friend of trump for a very long time. It was just recently that he started to separate himself. And even then he hasn't put much effort into that. There are no ethical billionaires. Remember this.
item.dc:creator: JustGridSquares
